---
title: "Results"
subtitle: "Complete comparison across all methods"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/combined_metrics.json").json()
```

## Validation era (2006-2015)

All results below are from the **rolling pseudo-real-time validation** with 120 monthly forecast origins.

### Summary table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof resultMetric = Inputs.radio(
  ["rmse", "mae", "mase"],
  {label: "Metric", value: "rmse"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const mk = `avg_${resultMetric}`;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const rows = metrics.methods.map(m => {
    const row = {Method: m.display_name, Category: m.category};
    for (const h of horizons) {
      row[`h=${h}`] = m.summary[String(h)]?.[mk]?.toFixed(3) || "—";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    sort: "h=12",
    columns: ["Method", "Category", ...horizons.map(h => `h=${h}`)],
    width: {Method: 180, Category: 90}
  });
}
```

### Heatmap

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-heatmap
//| fig-cap: "Performance heatmap — all methods, variables, and horizons. Darker green = better (lower error)."

{
  const mk = resultMetric;
  const vars = ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"];
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const data = metrics.methods.flatMap(m =>
    vars.flatMap(v =>
      horizons.map(h => {
        const val = m.metrics[v]?.[String(h)]?.[mk];
        return {
          method: m.display_name,
          variable: v.replace(/_/g, " "),
          horizon: h,
          value: val || null,
          label: `h=${h}`
        };
      })
    )
  ).filter(d => d.value != null);

  // Compute cell key for faceting
  const cells = data.map(d => ({
    ...d,
    cell: `${d.variable} h=${d.horizon}`
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 180,
    marginBottom: 50,
    padding: 0.05,
    x: {
      label: null,
      domain: vars.flatMap(v => horizons.map(h => `${v.replace(/_/g, " ")} h=${h}`)),
      tickRotate: -45,
      tickSize: 0
    },
    y: {
      label: null,
      domain: metrics.methods.map(m => m.display_name).reverse()
    },
    color: {
      scheme: "greens",
      reverse: true,
      label: mk.toUpperCase(),
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.cell(cells, {
        x: "cell",
        y: "method",
        fill: "value",
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.method}\n${d.variable} h=${d.horizon}\n${mk}: ${d.value.toFixed(4)}`
      })
    ]
  });
}
```


### Per-variable breakdown

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof detailVar = Inputs.select(
  ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  {label: "Variable", format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-detail-bars
//| fig-cap: "Method comparison for the selected variable across horizons."

{
  const mk = resultMetric;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];

  const data = metrics.methods.flatMap(m =>
    horizons.map(h => ({
      method: m.display_name,
      horizon: `h=${h}`,
      value: m.metrics[detailVar]?.[String(h)]?.[mk] || 0
    }))
  );

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {label: null},
    y: {label: mk.toUpperCase(), grid: true},
    fx: {label: null},
    color: {legend: true},
    marks: [
      Plot.barY(data, Plot.groupX({y: "identity"}, {
        x: "method",
        y: "value",
        fill: "method",
        fx: "horizon",
        tip: true
      }))
    ]
  });
}
```


## Validation-era findings

1. **ARIMA is the benchmark to beat** — best classical method at all horizons
2. **Zero-shot Chronos-2 is competitive at h=1** but deteriorates at longer horizons
3. **Random walk remains a strong baseline** especially at h=12

---

## Test era (2016+)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
testMetrics = FileAttachment("_data/test_metrics.json").json()
subperiodData = FileAttachment("_data/subperiod_metrics.json").json()
```

Out-of-sample evaluation on 111 monthly origins (2016-01 to 2025-03). The agent-tuned config was locked from the search — no further optimization.

### Full test-era comparison

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof testMetric = Inputs.radio(
  ["rmse", "mae", "mase"],
  {label: "Metric", value: "rmse"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const mk = `avg_${testMetric}`;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];
  const methods = testMetrics.methods.filter(m =>
    ["random_walk", "arima", "chronos2_zs", "chronos2_ft"].includes(m.name)
  );

  const rows = methods.map(m => {
    const row = {Method: m.display_name};
    for (const h of horizons) {
      row[`h=${h}`] = m.summary[String(h)]?.[mk]?.toFixed(3) || "—";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    sort: "h=12",
    columns: ["Method", ...horizons.map(h => `h=${h}`)],
    width: {Method: 200}
  });
}
```

### Subperiod breakdown

The test era spans three distinct macroeconomic regimes. Select a subperiod to see which methods handle each regime best.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedSubperiod = Inputs.select(
  ["pre_covid", "covid", "post_covid"],
  {label: "Subperiod", value: "pre_covid",
   format: v => ({"pre_covid": "Pre-COVID (2016-2019)", "covid": "COVID (2020-2021)", "post_covid": "Post-COVID / Inflation (2022+)"})[v]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-subperiod
//| fig-cap: "Average RMSE across target variables by method and horizon for the selected subperiod."

{
  const mk = testMetric;
  const horizons = [1, 3, 6, 12];
  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "chronos2_zs", "chronos2_ft"];
  const methodNames = {
    random_walk: "Random Walk", arima: "ARIMA",
    chronos2_zs: "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)", chronos2_ft: "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)"
  };

  const vars = ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"];

  // Compute avg across variables for the selected subperiod
  const data = methodOrder.flatMap(method => {
    return horizons.map(h => {
      const vals = vars.map(v => {
        const row = subperiodData.find(r =>
          r.method === method && r.subperiod === selectedSubperiod && r.variable === v && r.horizon === h
        );
        return row?.[mk];
      }).filter(v => v != null);
      return {
        method: methodNames[method],
        horizon: h,
        value: vals.length > 0 ? vals.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / vals.length : null
      };
    });
  }).filter(d => d.value != null);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Forecast horizon (months)", ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]},
    y: {label: `Average ${mk.toUpperCase()}`, grid: true},
    color: {
      domain: Object.values(methodNames),
      range: ["#95a5a6", "#2ecc71", "#9b59b6", "#e74c3c"]
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", fill: "method",
        r: 5, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

### Per-variable subperiod detail

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof testDetailVar = Inputs.select(
  ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  {label: "Variable", format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const mk = testMetric;
  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "chronos2_zs", "chronos2_ft"];
  const methodNames = {
    random_walk: "Random Walk", arima: "ARIMA",
    chronos2_zs: "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)", chronos2_ft: "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)"
  };

  const filtered = subperiodData.filter(r =>
    r.subperiod === selectedSubperiod && r.variable === testDetailVar && methodOrder.includes(r.method)
  ).map(r => ({
    method: methodNames[r.method],
    horizon: r.horizon,
    value: r[mk]
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 340,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Horizon", ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]},
    y: {label: mk.toUpperCase(), grid: true},
    color: {
      domain: Object.values(methodNames),
      range: ["#95a5a6", "#2ecc71", "#9b59b6", "#e74c3c"]
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.line(filtered, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(filtered, {
        x: "horizon", y: "value", fill: "method",
        r: 4, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```


## Test-era findings

1. **Random walk dominates** the full test era — regime changes (COVID, inflation) favor the naive baseline
2. **Zero-shot Chronos-2 beats ARIMA** at h=3, h=6, h=12 — the foundation model is more robust to structural breaks
3. **Agent-tuned config overfits** — covariates and fine-tuning from validation era hurt on the test era, especially at long horizons
4. **Pre-COVID (2016-19):** Zero-shot Chronos-2 is the best method — the agent-tuned config also does well
5. **COVID (2020-21):** Random walk wins — all models struggle with the unprecedented shock. Agent-tuned config helps for unemployment.
6. **Post-COVID (2022+):** ARIMA and random walk recover. Agent-tuned config is the worst — overfitting is most severe here
